Trail: perf: 13x faster semantic diff and tree-keyed snapshot cache
  Number:  4
  ID:      019f87a1-c3c9-7eb1-bbef-946aed304aec
  Branch:  perf/graph-cache-reuse
  Base:    main
  Status:  merged
  Author:  khaong
  URL:     https://entire.io/gh/entireio/entire-graph/trails/4
  Assignees: khaong
  Created: 2026-07-22T02:22:33Z
  Updated: 2026-07-22T12:00:24Z

Description:
Speed up the semantic-diff path and stop over-invalidating the snapshot cache. Dependents scan: git-grep prefilter + provider MaxParseBytes parity (3m17s -> 15s on entire-graph; 17.8s -> 0.3s on cli code commits). Snapshot cache: tree-only keying, serving commit re-stamped on load. Deliberate semantics changes: dependents_count excludes >4MiB files (provider parity); new additive warnings (E_FILE_TOO_LARGE, W_DEPENDENTS_PREFILTER_FAILED, E_PARSE_ERROR); same-tree rebase can serve prior-history FILE_CHANGES_WITH co-change edges (heuristic, accepted). Full writeup on PR #46.

content.text:
Review target: GitHub pull request `46`.

Gather this target's diff with (instead of any local `git diff`):
1. `gh pr view 46 --json title,body,author,baseRefName,headRefName,state,additions,deletions,changedFiles,labels` for context
2. `gh pr diff 46` for the unified diff

The PR's diff is the only review scope — local working-tree changes are out of scope. When you need surrounding code, Read the files in this checkout if it matches the PR's branch, otherwise fetch file contents via `gh`.

Analyze the changes and provide a thorough code review that includes:
- An overview of what the PR does
- Analysis of code quality and style
- Specific suggestions for improvements
- Any potential issues or risks

Keep your review concise but thorough. Focus on:
- Code correctness
- Following project conventions
- Performance implications
- Test coverage
- Security considerations

Format your review with clear sections and bullet points.

result.output:
{"additions":717,"author":{"id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM1MTkzNA==","is_bot":false,"login":"khaong","name":"Alex Ong"},"baseRefName":"main","body":"<!-- entire-trail-link-start -->
https://entire.io/gh/entireio/entire-graph/trails/4
<!-- entire-trail-link-end -->

## Summary

Two performance fixes for the graph plugin, found while timing `entire graph commit` on this repo (2m14s–3m45s per run):

1. **Dependents scan prefilter + size parity** (`internal/sem/dependents.go`): `buildReferenceIndex` parsed every supported file in the head tree on every semantic diff — including ~215 MB of vendored grammar `parser.c` files that the provider snapshot path refuses to parse (`MaxParseBytes`, 4 MiB). Now: candidate files are prefiltered with `git grep` over the changed names (binary-inclusive, so it's a strict superset of the whole-token match — counts for real identifiers are bit-identical), and files over the provider's default parse cap are skipped with an `E_FILE_TOO_LARGE` warning, matching the graph's own eligibility everywhere else.

   **Measured: `entire graph commit d7204db` 3m17s → 15s (~13x).**

2. **Tree-keyed snapshot cache** (`internal/sem/search_cache.go`): the cache key and validation pinned the commit SHA as well as the tree, so empty commits, amends, and rebases invalidated a byte-identical graph. Now keyed and validated on tree only, with the serving commit re-stamped onto loaded snapshots (`Header.Commit` doubles as the treeish for query-time content reads, so this is load-bearing, not cosmetic). Verified end-to-end: `index --head` → `git commit --allow-empty` → search reports `cache-hit` with the new HEAD.

## Deliberate semantics changes (flagged for review)

- `dependents_count` no longer counts entities in files >4 MiB — parity with the provider, which already excludes them from every other output. Skips are reported as warnings rather than silent.
- Full-profile snapshots embed history-derived `FILE_CHANGES_WITH` co-change relations; a same-tree cache hit after a **rebase** can serve those edges from the prior history. Accepted because they're heuristic and confidence-scored (0.55–0.7); documented in `searchSnapshotKey`'s comment.
- New (additive) `warnings` entries consumers may start seeing from the diff commands: `E_FILE_TOO_LARGE` (oversized file skipped during dependents counting), `W_DEPENDENTS_PREFILTER_FAILED` (grep prefilter failed, full scan used), and `E_PARSE_ERROR`/`E_PARSE_TIMEOUT` (candidate file didn't parse cleanly; previously silent undercount). Nothing removed or renamed.
- Old commit-keyed cache entries become unreachable orphans in the v4 cache dir (no correctness impact; they'll sit until a cleanup pass exists).

## Testing

- TDD throughout: grep-prefilter superset matrix (case/substring/self-exclusion), binary-flagged file inclusion (red→green), oversized-skip + warning, grep-failure fallback + warning, tree-reuse across commits at preindex and search level, OnlyFiles derivation across commits.
- `mise run check` (gofmt, vet, race tests, build) green.
- Four-agent review (code, tests, silent-failures, comments) applied: binary `-I` hole fixed, observability warnings added, stale comments corrected.

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Tree-keyed caching and commit re-stamping affect search/preindex correctness and content reads; dependents prefilter changes which files are scanned and when counts can be under-reported (with warnings).
> 
> **Overview**
> **Speeds semantic diff and snapshot reuse** by narrowing dependent-reference work and reusing parsed graphs when only the commit SHA changes.
> 
> **Dependents (`dependents.go`)** no longer walks every supported file on HEAD. It preselects candidates with **`git grep`** (binary-inclusive via new **`GrepTreePathsIncludingBinary`**), skips files over the provider’s default **`MaxParseBytes`**, and on grep failure falls back to a full-tree scan with **`W_DEPENDENTS_PREFILTER_FAILED`**. Oversized skips and parse failures surface **`E_FILE_TOO_LARGE`** / parse warnings on **`Result.Warnings`**; whole-token counting semantics are unchanged.
> 
> **Search cache (`search_cache.go`, `search.go`)** keys and validates snapshots on **tree** only (not commit). Loaded entries **re-stamp `Header.Commit`** to the serving HEAD so content reads and API responses stay correct. Race checks during search compare **tree** identity; preindexed grep preselection no longer requires commit equality.
> 
> **Semantics to note:** `dependents_count` excludes files above the parse cap (aligned with the provider). Same-tree cache hits after a rebase may reuse history-derived **`FILE_CHANGES_WITH`** edges from when the entry was built.
> 
> <sup>Reviewed by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/bugbot) for commit 95f746d6a4a103fd815fd73259f8f4099946ddfc. Configure [here](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard/bugbot).</sup>

content.text:
# Review: Trail #4 — perf: 13x faster semantic diff and tree-keyed snapshot cache (PR #46, merged)

**Verdict: solid work.** The two optimizations are correct, the deliberate semantics changes are honestly flagged in the PR body, and the test coverage is unusually thorough. I verified the key claims against the code and ran the touched packages (`internal/sem`, `internal/gitutil`) — all green. I found no correctness bugs; the notes below are minor polish items.

## What the PR does

1. **Dependents prefilter** (`internal/sem/dependents.go`): `buildReferenceIndex` used to parse every supported file in the head tree per semantic diff. Now candidates are narrowed with `git grep` over the changed names and files over the provider's 4 MiB parse cap are skipped — 3m17s → 15s on this repo.
2. **Tree-keyed snapshot cache** (`internal/sem/search_cache.go`): cache key and validation drop the commit SHA, so empty commits/amends/rebases no longer invalidate a byte-identical graph; the serving commit is re-stamped on load.

## What I verified holds up

- **The superset guarantee is real and well-defended.** Case-insensitive fixed-string substring match strictly contains the case-sensitive whole-token check, and the `-I` binary hole (git silently dropping NUL-containing source from grep results) was caught and closed with `GrepTreePathsIncludingBinary`, pinned by a red→green test at both the gitutil and dependents level.
- **Grep exit code 1 (no matches) is handled as an empty result** (`git.go:186-189`), not an error — so a zero-match name set doesn't spuriously trigger the slow fallback or its warning.
- **Provider parity claims are accurate**: `E_FILE_TOO_LARGE` / `E_PARSE_ERROR` / `E_PARSE_TIMEOUT` reuse the provider's exact codes and `"warning"` severity (`provider.go:827-885`).
- **The commit re-stamp is genuinely load-bearing**, as claimed: `Header.Commit` feeds `openSearchContentReader` as the treeish for query-time content reads (`search.go:367-369`), so serving a stale commit could break content reads, not just provenance display.
- **Failure paths are no longer silent**: grep failure → full-scan fallback + `W_DEPENDENTS_PREFILTER_FAILED`; oversized skips and parse failures each warn. The fallback test (poisoning exec with a NUL-byte pattern) is a clever, deterministic way to force the failure branch.
- **Tests cover the right matrix**: case/substring/self-exclusion semantics, empty-names short-circuit, size boundary on both sides, binary-flagged inclusion, and tree-reuse re-stamping at all three levels (preindex, search, OnlyFiles derivation).

## Minor findings (nothing blocking; all low severity)

1. **"Parity with the provider's eligibility" is size-only.** The provider also skips minified files (`E_MINIFIED`, `provider.go:842`) and non-default-build Go files; the dependents scan still parses those. Behavior is unchanged from before the PR, but the comment at `dependents.go:172` slightly overstates the parity. Worth a clause if anyone touches it again.
2. **Warning noise in the fallback path.** When grep fails, the full-tree scan emits `E_FILE_TOO_LARGE` for *every* oversized supported file — on a repo like this one with ~215 MB of vendored `parser.c`, that's dozens of warnings for files irrelevant to the changed names, whereas the prefiltered path only warns on actual candidates. Rare path, but it makes the two paths' warning output inconsistent.
3. **Stale wording in provenance error messages.** `PreindexProviderSnapshot`'s mismatch error still says "want commit %q" (`search_cache.go` ~line 768) even though commit no longer participates in the check — a differing commit is now explicitly *not* a mismatch. Same cosmetic issue in `selectiveSearchSnapshotFromFull`.
4. **Cache orphans acknowledged but unresolved.** Old commit-keyed entries sit unreachable in the same `search-snapshot-v4` directory. Since the key layout changed, bumping to `v5` would have put new entries in a fresh dir, making the eventual cleanup a simple "delete old version dirs" — worth considering when the cleanup pass gets built.
5. **Tiny race window in the no-hit path** (`search.go:302`): the cached snapshot's commit is re-stamped to HEAD *at load time*; if a same-tree commit lands between load and preselection, the response reports the slightly older commit. Content-identical trees, so it's benign — noting for completeness only.

The trail itself has no open findings, and given the PR is merged and everything above is polish-level, I'd say none of these warrant reopening it. Happy to file items 1–4 as trail findings (`entire trail finding add`) if you want them tracked for a follow-up.
Open a new trail and address the minor findings
I'll open a new trail on a fresh branch and fix findings 1–4. First, let me check how trail creation works.

input.command:
entire trail create --branch chore/pr46-review-followups --base main --checkout --title "chore(sem): follow-up polish from PR #46 review" --body "Address minor findings from the review of trail #4 (PR #46):

1. Correct the provider-parity comment in dependents.go — parity is size-only (provider also skips minified and non-default-build Go files; dependents scan does not).
2. Reduce warning noise in the grep-fallback path: only emit E_FILE_TOO_LARGE for files that would have been grep candidates (contain a changed name), keeping the two paths' warning output consistent.
3. Fix stale provenance error wording in PreindexProviderSnapshot and selectiveSearchSnapshotFromFull — commit no longer participates in those checks.
4. Bump search snapshot cache version to v5 so tree-keyed entries live in a fresh dir and orphaned commit-keyed v4 entries can be cleaned up by deleting old version dirs."

content.text: Full check is green. Committing and pushing to the trail branch:

input.command:
git add internal/sem/dependents.go internal/sem/dependents_test.go internal/sem/search_cache.go && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
chore(sem): follow-up polish from PR #46 review

Address the minor findings from the trail #4 review:

- Correct the provider-parity comment on the dependents size skip: parity
  is size-only; the provider additionally skips minified files and
  non-default-build Go files, which the dependents scan still parses,
  exactly as it did before the prefilter existed.
- Stop the grep-failure fallback from spraying warnings about
  non-candidates: the full-tree scan now applies the prefilter's
  case-insensitive substring test in-process before emitting
  E_FILE_TOO_LARGE or parse-failure warnings, so a huge vendored blob
  containing no changed name no longer warns. Counting is unchanged.
- Reword the preindex and selective-derivation provenance mismatch
  errors, which still implied commit equality was checked after the
  tree-only keying change made commit irrelevant to both checks.
- Bump the snapshot cache version to search-snapshot-v5 so tree-keyed
  entries live in a fresh directory and the commit-keyed v4 orphans can
  be cleaned up by deleting old version dirs, not per-entry analysis.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git push -u origin chore/pr46-review-followups
input.description: Commit follow-up fixes and push branch
